relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks the burdens of sacred transport.
Kohath is counted by clan and household.
Those in the service years enter the work.
Kohath carries the most holy things.
Aaron and sons first cover the ark.
The ark is layered and prepared for carrying.
The table of presence is covered with its vessels and bread.
Scarlet and leather protect the table.
The lampstand and tools are wrapped in blue.
The lampstand is placed on its carrying frame.
The gold altar is covered and poled.
The service vessels are wrapped and carried safely.
The altar is cleared and covered.
Its tools are gathered beneath protective covering.
Only after covering may Kohath carry without touching the holy.
Eleazar oversees oil, incense, offering, anointing, and sanctuary vessels.
YHWH speaks for the preservation of Kohath.
The Kohathite clans must not be cut off.
Clear assignment keeps them alive near the holy.
They do not enter to gaze on the holy things.
YHWH speaks the Gershonite count.
Gershon is counted by household and clan.
Those in service years enter the work.
Gershon serves through carrying coverings.
They carry tent fabrics and entrance screen.
They carry courtyard hangings, cords, and tools.
Aaron and sons assign every burden.
Ithamar oversees the Gershonite service.
Merari is counted by clan and household.
Those in service years enter the work.
Merari carries frames, bars, pillars, and sockets.
Every item is assigned by name.
Ithamar oversees the Merarite service.
Kohath is counted for service.
The service-age workers are gathered.
Their number is recorded.
Kohath's workers are counted by YHWH's command.
Gershon is counted for service.
The service-age workers are gathered.
Their number is recorded.
Gershon's workers are counted by YHWH's command.
Merari is counted for service.
The service-age workers are gathered.
Their number is recorded.
Merari's workers are counted by YHWH's command.
All Levite transport workers are gathered.
Each enters service and burden according to assignment.
The full number is recorded.
Each person is counted according to service and burden.

contemplative_note: Numbers 4 makes sacred work survivable through covering, sequencing, clear assignment, and distributed burden. The holy is not an excuse to endanger workers; the text explicitly orders the work so they may live.
